body.title: Instrument memory retrieval with freshness metadata and per-call cost telemetry
body.result: memory_search: latency_ms + node_age_days added; pe_chain basket telemetry deferred (pe_chain.py not in current UU codebase)
body.description:
From URL 16 (context architecture): four questions every retrieval call should answer — what does it need to know, how fresh must it be, who can access it, what does it cost. Currently burn rate tracks total spend but not per-retrieval latency, freshness of retrieved content, or per-call accumulation. Add: latency + result count to memory_search telemetry; node_age_days field in results; retrieval cost accumulation in pe_chain basket close telemetry. Enables the four-question framework as a monitoring surface.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/memory_search.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database, theigors/rules/coding
**Scope boundary:** In scope: telemetry fields on retrieval; freshness metadata in results. Out of scope: enforcing freshness thresholds (observation only), changing retrieval logic.
**Test plan:** Run memory_search; verify result objects include node_age_days field. Run pe_chain; verify close telemetry includes total_retrieval_calls and avg_retrieval_latency_ms.
